steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Apply the volume formula for a triangular prism
steps.content: We have the formula: $$V = Bh$$ Given: $V = 315\text{ cm}^3$ and $h = 9\text{ cm}$. Substituting these values into the formula: $$315 = B \cdot 9$$

steps.title: Solve for the area of the base
steps.content: Divide both sides by 9 to isolate $B$: $$B = \frac{315}{9} = 35$$ The area of the base is 35 cm^2.
